title: orlaya grandiflora var. pusilla
intro: Orlaya grandiflora var. pusilla, also known as the small-flowered caraway or lesser Alexanders, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Orlaya grandiflora var. pusilla include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, improve digestive health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
